cli_observability_and_fix.name: CLI Observability and Auto-Fix for signet-eval
cli_observability_and_fix.description:
Single-module implementation scoped to src/main.rs (plus integration tests in tests/integration_cli.rs). Three cohesive changes to the existing CLI command dispatch:

1. **validate --fix / --dry-run**: Add `--fix` and `--dry-run` flags to the existing Validate CLI variant. When `--fix` is set, call `fix_policy()` (which returns a new Policy), diff against the original. If no changes, exit 0. If changes exist and `--dry-run`, print human-readable diff and exit 0 without writing. Otherwise write the new policy file; if vault is initialized, re-sign (rollback on signing failure). If vault absent, skip re-signing. `--dry-run` without `--fix` is a hard error (exit 1). Structural errors are passed through as diagnostics unchanged — only mechanical clamping results from fix_policy() are applied (C016). Operation is idempotent (C014).

2. **enable clears all overrides**: Modify the `enable` command handler so that when invoked without `--session`, it clears global disable state AND iterates `list_disabled_sessions()` to clear each session disable. Accumulate cleared items and print each. If nothing was disabled, print 'Not currently disabled.' and exit 0.

3. **status shows enforcement state**: Extend the `status` command handler to query `is_paused_file()`, `pause_until_file()`, `list_pauses()`, `list_disabled_sessions()`, and global disable state. Render each category as a plain-text section with headers matching existing CLI output patterns. Omit sections with no active overrides. Must work without a vault present (C019).

All three features share the same CLI arg parsing infrastructure, the same error handling patterns, and the same data model (Policy, Vault, pause/disable state files). No independent subsystems — just three extensions to existing match arms in main.rs.

contract.name: CLI Observability and Auto-Fix for signet-eval
contract.description: Single-module implementation scoped to src/main.rs (plus integration tests in tests/integration_cli.rs). Three cohesive extensions to existing CLI command dispatch: (1) validate --fix / --dry-run for mechanical policy auto-correction with atomic write and optional vault re-signing; (2) enable command that clears global disable state AND all per-session disables; (3) status command that queries and renders all enforcement override/pause state as plain-text sections. All features share CLI arg parsing infrastructure, error handling patterns, and the Policy/Vault/pause-disable data model. No unsafe code; deterministic policy evaluation; exit code always 0 in hook mode.

types.name: PolicyPath

fields.name: value
fields.description: Absolute or relative path to the policy file.

validators.error_message: Policy path must not be empty.
Policy path must end with .toml or .yaml.

types.description: Validated filesystem path to a policy file. Must be non-empty and end with .toml or .yaml.

types.name: SessionId
types.description: Opaque string identifier for an evaluation session.

types.name: RuleId

fields.name: value
fields.description: Rule identifier string.

validators.error_message: RuleId must be alphanumeric, underscores, or hyphens.

types.description: Identifier for a policy rule. Non-empty, alphanumeric with underscores/hyphens.

types.name: FieldPath

fields.name: value
fields.description: Dot-delimited field path, e.g. 'rules.max_tokens.limit'.

validators.error_message: FieldPath must contain only alphanumeric, dots, underscores, and bracket notation.

types.description: Dot-delimited path to a field within a policy rule configuration.

types.name: Timestamp

fields.name: value
fields.description: RFC 3339 formatted UTC timestamp.

validators.error_message: Timestamp must be RFC 3339 UTC (ending in Z).

types.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp string.

types.name: ValidateMode
types.description: Operational mode for the validate command.

types.name: CliExitCode
types.description: Well-known CLI exit codes for all commands.

types.name: Policy

fields.name: path
fields.description: Filesystem location from which the policy was loaded.

fields.name: rules
fields.description: Ordered list of enforcement rules in this policy.

fields.name: raw_bytes
fields.description: Original serialized policy content for diffing.

types.description: Deserialized policy document governing evaluation constraints and rules.

types.name: DiagnosticSeverity
types.description: Severity level of a validation diagnostic.

types.name: Diagnostic

fields.name: severity
fields.description: Severity level of this diagnostic.

fields.name: rule_id
fields.description: Constraint ID this diagnostic pertains to (e.g. 'C016').

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable diagnostic message.

fields.name: fixable
fields.description: True if this diagnostic is mechanically fixable (not structural).

types.description: A single validation diagnostic emitted by validate, with optional fix hint.

types.name: DiagnosticList
types.description: Ordered collection of Diagnostic entries from a validation pass.

types.name: FixChange

fields.name: rule_id
fields.description: Constraint ID that triggered this fix.

fields.name: field_path
fields.description: Dot-delimited path to the field that was changed.

fields.name: old_value
fields.description: Serialized previous value.

fields.name: new_value
fields.description: Serialized replacement value.

types.description: A single mechanical change applied by fix_policy().

types.name: FixChangeList
types.description: Ordered collection of FixChange entries.

types.name: FixResult

fields.name: original
fields.description: The policy before fixes were applied.

fields.name: fixed
fields.description: The policy after mechanical clamping fixes.

fields.name: changes
fields.description: List of individual changes applied.

fields.name: has_changes
fields.description: True if fixed differs from original (convenience flag).

types.description: Result of calling fix_policy(): the corrected policy and a summary of changes.

types.name: ClearedOverrideKind
types.description: Discriminant for what type of override was cleared by enable.

types.name: ClearedOverride

fields.name: kind
fields.description: What kind of override was cleared.

fields.name: session_id
fields.description: Session ID if this was a session-level disable.

types.description: A single override that was cleared by the enable command.

types.name: ClearedOverrideList
types.description: Collection of overrides cleared by a single enable invocation.

types.name: EnableResult

fields.name: cleared
fields.description: Overrides that were cleared.

fields.name: was_disabled
fields.description: True if anything was actually disabled before enable ran.

types.description: Result of the enable command: what was cleared, or nothing.

types.name: SessionIdList
types.description: List of session identifiers.

types.name: PauseScope
types.description: Scope discriminant for a PauseEntry.

types.name: PauseEntry

fields.name: scope
fields.description: What this pause applies to.

fields.name: rule_id
fields.description: If scope is Rule or RuleSession, the rule ID.

fields.name: session_id
fields.description: If scope is Session or RuleSession, the session ID.

fields.name: expires_at
fields.description: When this pause expires; None means indefinite.

types.description: A single active pause record (global, per-rule, or per-session).

types.name: PauseEntryList
types.description: Ordered collection of active PauseEntry records.

types.name: EnforcementState

fields.name: globally_disabled

fields.name: disabled_sessions

fields.name: global_pause

fields.name: active_pauses

fields.name: vault_present

types.name: DiffLine

fields.name: field_path

fields.name: old_value

fields.name: new_value
fields.description: Corrected value as string.

functions.name: derive_validate_mode
functions.description: Derives the ValidateMode from the parsed CLI flags --fix and --dry-run. Enforces the constraint that --dry-run without --fix is a hard error (exit 1). Pure function with no side effects.

error_cases.name: dry_run_without_fix
error_cases.condition: dry_run is true but fix is false

preconditions: fix and dry_run are booleans parsed from CLI args
postconditions: If !fix && !dry_run → DiagnoseOnly
If fix && dry_run → DryRun
If fix && !dry_run → Fix
If !fix && dry_run → error (InvalidArguments)

functions.name: fix_policy
functions.description: Pure function: applies mechanical clamping fixes to a policy (C016). Returns a FixResult containing the corrected policy, the list of changes, and whether any changes were made. Only mechanical clamping is applied; structural errors are not modified. Deterministic and side-effect-free.
preconditions: policy is a valid deserialized policy object (may contain clamping violations but not parse errors)
postconditions: has_changes == !changes.is_empty()
If has_changes is false, fixed_policy is semantically identical to input policy
Each FixChange in changes has distinct (rule_id, field_path) pair
All changes represent mechanical clamping only — no structural modifications
Function is deterministic: same input always produces same output

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n = number of rules in policy

functions.name: handle_validate
functions.description: Handler for the validate command match arm. Runs existing validation to produce diagnostics, then branches on ValidateMode: DiagnoseOnly emits diagnostics and exits; DryRun computes fix and renders structured diff without writing; Fix computes fix, writes via atomic tempfile, re-signs if vault present (rollback on signing failure), and emits remaining unfixable diagnostics. Idempotent (C014).

inputs.name: policy_path
inputs.description: Path to the policy file to validate/fix.

inputs.name: mode
inputs.description: Derived validate mode (DiagnoseOnly, DryRun, or Fix).

error_cases.name: policy_file_not_found
error_cases.condition: policy_path does not exist on the filesystem

error_cases.name: tempfile_write_failure
error_cases.condition: Cannot create or write to tempfile during Fix mode

error_data.message: Failed to write temporary policy file

error_cases.name: signing_failure
error_cases.condition: Vault re-signing fails after writing fixed policy

error_data.message: Failed to re-sign policy; changes rolled back

error_cases.name: persist_failure
error_cases.condition: Atomic rename of tempfile over original fails

error_data.message: Failed to persist fixed policy file

preconditions: policy_path points to an existing, readable file
mode has been validated by derive_validate_mode (no InvalidArguments state)
postconditions: DiagnoseOnly: diagnostics emitted to stdout, no filesystem writes
DryRun: structured diff emitted to stdout, no filesystem writes, exit Success
Fix with no changes: 'No fixable issues' emitted, exit Success, no writes
Fix with changes and vault present: policy written atomically, re-signed, exit Success
Fix with changes and vault absent: policy written atomically, re-signing skipped, exit Success
Fix with changes and signing failure: tempfile deleted, exit InternalError, original policy unchanged
Diagnostics with fixable=false are always passed through unchanged
side_effects: Writes corrected policy via atomic tempfile in Fix mode
Calls vault re-sign in Fix mode when vault is present
Prints diagnostics and/or diff to stdout

structured_side_effects.description: Reads the policy file for validation and fix input.
Writes corrected policy in Fix mode. Atomic: tempfile created, then renamed over original.
Re-signs the fixed policy via vault when vault is present. Not a network call per se but an external process/library invocation.
Emits diagnostics, diff, or status messages to stdout.

functions.name: handle_enable
functions.description: Handler for the enable command match arm (when invoked without --session). Clears global disable state, then iterates list_disabled_sessions() to clear each session disable. Accumulates cleared items into EnableResult. If nothing was disabled, prints 'Not currently disabled.' and exits 0. Otherwise prints each cleared item and exits 0. Best-effort: errors clearing individual sessions are accumulated but do not halt processing.

error_cases.name: state_dir_inaccessible
error_cases.condition: Cannot read the disable state directory

error_data.message: Failed to access enforcement state directory

error_cases.name: session_clear_failure
error_cases.condition: One or more per-session disable files could not be removed

error_data.message: Failed to clear one or more session disables

postconditions: Global disable state file is removed if it existed
All per-session disable state files are removed if they existed
If no overrides were active, 'Not currently disabled.' is printed to stdout
If overrides were cleared, each cleared override is printed to stdout
Exit code is always Success (0)
side_effects: Prints cleared override list to stdout

structured_side_effects.description: Removes the global disable state file if present.
Removes each per-session disable state file.
Prints cleared overrides or 'Not currently disabled.' message.

functions.name: handle_status
functions.description: Handler for the status command match arm. Queries all enforcement override and pause state: global disable, per-session disables, global pause (is_paused_file, pause_until_file), and all active pauses (list_pauses). Constructs EnforcementState and renders sorted, section-based plain text to stdout. Sections with no active overrides are omitted. Must work without a vault present (C019).

error_cases.name: state_query_failure
error_cases.condition: Cannot read one or more state files or directories

postconditions: EnforcementState is fully populated from filesystem queries
Sections with no active overrides are not printed
Output uses existing CLI output header style (plain text, section headers)
Pauses within each section are sorted by scope then expiry
vault_present reflects actual vault state; absence does not cause error
Exit code is always Success (0)
side_effects: Reads vault metadata for presence check

structured_side_effects.description: Enumerates and reads per-session disable state files.
Reads is_paused_file, pause_until_file, and list_pauses results.
Checks vault directory/metadata for presence. Does not require vault to be initialized.
Renders enforcement state sections to stdout.

functions.name: render_fix_diff
functions.description: Pure function: renders a FixChangeList into a human-readable structured diff for --dry-run output. Each line formatted as 'rule_id: field_path: old → new'. Output order matches change list order.
preconditions: changes is a valid FixChangeList (possibly empty)
postconditions: Output is a newline-delimited string with one line per change
If changes is empty, output is the empty string
Each line matches format: '{rule_id}: {field_path}: {old_value} → {new_value}'

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n = number of changes

functions.name: render_enforcement_state
functions.description: Pure function: renders an EnforcementState into plain-text sectioned output matching existing CLI output patterns. Omits sections with no active overrides. Sections: 'Enforcement' (globally disabled), 'Disabled Sessions' (list), 'Global Pause' (until timestamp), 'Active Pauses' (list with scope/rule/session/expiry). Pauses sorted by scope then expiry.
postconditions: Output is a newline-delimited string with section headers and content
Sections with no active overrides are omitted entirely
If all sections empty, output is 'No active overrides.'
Pauses within Active Pauses section sorted by scope (Global < Rule < Session) then expiry ascending
Section headers match existing CLI output style

performance_budget.complexity: O(n log n) where n = number of pause entries (due to sorting)

functions.name: build_enable_result
functions.description: Pure function: constructs an EnableResult from the global disable state and list of cleared session overrides. Determines was_disabled from whether any overrides were cleared.

inputs.name: global_was_disabled
inputs.description: Whether global disable was active before clearing.

inputs.name: cleared_sessions
inputs.description: List of session IDs whose disables were cleared.

postconditions: was_disabled == (global_was_disabled || !cleared_sessions.is_empty())
cleared list contains GlobalDisable entry iff global_was_disabled
cleared list contains one SessionDisable entry per session in cleared_sessions
Order: GlobalDisable first (if present), then SessionDisable entries in input order

functions.name: query_enforcement_state
functions.description: Queries all enforcement state from the filesystem: global disable, per-session disables, global pause, pause-until, all active pauses, and vault presence. Aggregates into EnforcementState. Vault absence is not an error (C019).

error_cases.name: state_dir_read_failure
error_cases.condition: Cannot read the state directory or its contents

error_data.message: Failed to read enforcement state from filesystem

postconditions: All fields of EnforcementState are populated from current filesystem state
vault_present is false if vault directory does not exist or is uninitialized
active_pauses includes only non-expired pauses at time of query
disabled_sessions contains exactly the sessions returned by list_disabled_sessions()
side_effects: Reads enforcement state files from disk

invariants: C014: handle_validate in Fix mode is idempotent — running fix on an already-fixed policy produces no changes and no writes.
C016: fix_policy() applies only mechanical clamping fixes; structural errors are never modified and pass through as diagnostics with fixable=false.
C019: handle_status and query_enforcement_state must succeed without a vault present; vault_present=false is a normal state, not an error.
No unsafe code in any implementation.
All errors on user input paths are handled — no unwrap() on fallible operations derived from user input.
Exit code is always 0 in hook mode, enforced at outermost CLI dispatch level.
Policy evaluation (including fix_policy) is deterministic and side-effect-free.
Atomic write semantics: in Fix mode, policy is written to tempfile first, signature computed, then tempfile renamed over original. On signing failure, tempfile is deleted and original is untouched.
Signing order: vault re-sign is attempted BEFORE atomic rename of tempfile over original policy. Rename only occurs on signing success.
handle_enable is idempotent: clearing already-cleared state is a no-op that reports 'Not currently disabled.'
render_enforcement_state omits sections with no active overrides; if all sections empty, outputs 'No active overrides.'
All 128 existing tests continue to pass; new features have integration tests in tests/integration_cli.rs.

data_access.rationale: Reads policy files (INTERNAL) to validate and optionally fix rule configurations. Reads and writes pause/disable state files (INTERNAL) to query and clear enforcement overrides. Reads vault metadata (INTERNAL) to determine vault presence and perform re-signing of fixed policies. Writes policy files (INTERNAL) when --fix mode persists corrected policy. All data is local filesystem state with no PII; classification is INTERNAL throughout.

side_effects.rationale: Reads policy file for validation/fix, pause and disable state files for status/enable queries, and vault directory for presence check and re-signing.
Writes corrected policy via atomic tempfile persist in --fix mode. Deletes global and per-session disable state files in enable mode.
Re-signs the fixed policy file when vault is present, ensuring cryptographic integrity is maintained after auto-fix.
